title: Free Fire Launches in India
detail: Garena Free Fire officially launches on the Indian App Store and Google Play, beginning an era of battle royale gaming that would transform Indian mobile esports.

title: First Community Tournaments Emerge
detail: Grassroots community-organized Free Fire tournaments appear on social media and Discord servers across India, with prize pools funded by players themselves.

title: Total Gaming Esports Founded
detail: Ajjubhai officially establishes Total Gaming Esports, combining his growing YouTube presence with competitive ambitions.

title: Neyoo Squad Founded
detail: Legendary sniper Neyoo establishes his own squad to compete in the emerging Indian Free Fire competitive scene.

title: Team Elite Founded
detail: Team Elite is established in Kolkata, beginning their journey as one of India's most consistent esports organizations.

title: FFIC 2019 Season 1 Begins
detail: Garena India launches the first Free Fire India Championship, marking the beginning of organized competitive Free Fire esports in the country.

title: Total Gaming Esports Win Inaugural FFIC
detail: Total Gaming Esports, led by Ajjubhai, become the first-ever FFIC champions in a dominant grand finals performance in Mumbai.

title: Galaxy Racer India Established
detail: The international Galaxy Racer organization launches their Indian Free Fire division, injecting serious organizational support into the scene.

title: Total Gaming Esports Defend FFIC Title
detail: Despite the COVID-19 pandemic forcing the tournament fully online, Total Gaming Esports successfully defended their championship, becoming the first back-to-back FFIC champions.

title: Blind Esports Founded by Raistar
detail: Raistar establishes Blind Esports with a vision of creating India's most mechanically gifted Free Fire roster.

title: Galaxy Racer India Claim First FFIC Title
detail: Galaxy Racer India defeats Total Gaming Esports in a historic grand finals, ending TGE's championship reign. K18 wins MVP.

title: Neyoo Transfers to Total Gaming Esports
detail: Star sniper Neyoo makes a landmark transfer from his own squad to Total Gaming Esports, disbanding Neyoo Squad in the process.

title: Total Gaming Esports Win Third FFIC Title
detail: Ajjubhai leads TGE to an unprecedented third FFIC title, winning MVP for the second time and cementing the organization as a dynasty.

title: Neyoo Squad Officially Disbanded
detail: With Neyoo firmly established at TGE, his original squad is formally disbanded, closing the chapter on one of India's earliest competitive FF teams.

title: Blind Esports Win First Major Title — FFSCC 2022
detail: Raistar and Blind Esports claim their first major championship at the Free Fire Skyler Circuit Championship, ending their status as nearly team. Raistar wins MVP.

title: Galaxy Racer India Claim Second FFIC Title
detail: Galaxy Racer India defeat Blind Esports in the FFIC 2022 S1 grand finals, with K18 winning his second MVP award. Record prize pool of ₹75L achieved.

title: Desi Gamers Squad Officially Disbanded
detail: Inaam announces the official disbanding of Desi Gamers Squad as he shifts focus entirely to content creation.

title: Blind Esports Win FFIC 2023 S1
detail: In front of over 2 million concurrent online viewers, Blind Esports claim the FFIC 2023 title. Raistar wins his second MVP, the highest-viewed Free Fire India event ever.

title: YoloStar Gang Win Inaugural FFIC 2024 Title
detail: In a historic upset, YoloStar Gang claim their first-ever FFIC title in a stunning comeback. Lokesh Gamer wins MVP. Prize pool crosses ₹1 Crore for the first time.

title: FFIC 2025 Season Announced
detail: Garena India officially announces the FFIC 2025 season with a record ₹1.5 Crore prize pool and expanded 36-team format.
